Search route handler with DFS (Distributed Frequency Search) support.\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path};\nuse axum::http::{HeaderMap, StatusCode};\nuse axum::response::Response;\nuse axum::Json;\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\nuse miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse miroir_core::idempotency::{QueryFingerprint, canonicalize_json};\nuse miroir_core::merger::ScoreMergeStrategy;\nuse miroir_core::replica_selection::SelectionObserver;\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{\n    dfs_query_then_fetch_search, plan_search_scatter, plan_search_scatter_for_group, plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor, SearchRequest, NodeClient,\n};\nuse miroir_core::session_pinning::WaitStrategy;\nuse serde::Deserialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tracing::{debug, error, info, info_span, instrument, warn};\n\nuse crate::middleware::SessionId;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::{AppState, parse_rate_limit};\n\n/// Metrics observer for replica selection events.\n///\n/// Reports selection scores and exploration events to Prometheus.\nstruct MetricsObserver {\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n}\n\nimpl SelectionObserver for MetricsObserver {\n    fn report_selection(&self, node_id: &str, score: f64) {\n        self.metrics.set_replica_selection_score(node_id, score);\n    }\n\n    fn report_exploration(&self) {\n        self.metrics.inc_replica_selection_exploration();\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hash a value for logging (obfuscates sensitive data like IPs).\nfn hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n}\n\n/// Node client implementation using the HTTP client.\n///\n/// Wraps each node call with Prometheus metrics recording for\n/// `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds` and `miroir_node_errors_total`.\npub struct ProxyNodeClient {\n    client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n    replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n}\n\nimpl ProxyNodeClient {\n    pub fn new(\n        client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n        metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n        replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self { client, metrics, replica_selector }\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\nimpl NodeClient for ProxyNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.search_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"search\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &miroir_core::scatter::PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.preflight_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"preflight\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n}\n\nfn error_label(e: &miroir_core::scatter::NodeError) -> &'static str {\n    match e {\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(_) => \"network\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { .. } => \"http\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => \"timeout\",\n    }\n}\n\npub fn router<S>() -> axum::Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(search_handler))\n}\n\n/// Search request body.\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\nstruct SearchRequestBody {\n    q: Option<String>,\n    offset: Option<usize>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    filter: Option<Value>,\n    facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScore\")]\n    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    rest: Value,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"offset\", &self.offset)\n            .field(\"limit\", &self.limit)\n            .field(\"filter\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"facets\", &self.facets)\n            .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n///\n/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n///\n/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n/// explicitly requested it.\n///\n/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn search_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<SessionId>>,\n    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It's an alias\n            (index.clone(), targets)\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias - use index directly\n            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n        }\n    } else {\n        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n    };\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None, None)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n        debug!(\n            session_id = ?sid,\n            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n            strategy = label,\n            \"session pinning applied\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Query coalescing (plan §13.10): Check for identical in-flight queries\n    // Skip for multi-target aliases (each target is different)\n    if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n        // Build fingerprint from canonicalized query body + index + settings version\n        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n        let query_body = serde_json::to_value(&body).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n        let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint::new(effective_index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n        // Try to coalesce with an existing in-flight query\n        if let Some(mut rx) = state.query_coalescer.try_coalesce(fingerprint.clone()).await {\n            // Successfully subscribed to an in-flight query - wait for its result\n            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_hits();\n            debug!(\n                index = %effective_index,\n                \"query coalesced: waiting for in-flight query result\"\n            );\n\n            // Wait for the response (with timeout matching the scatter timeout)\n            let timeout = Duration::from_millis(state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms);\n            let response_bytes = tokio::time::timeout(timeout, async move {\n                rx.recv().await\n            }).await;\n\n            match response_bytes {\n                Ok(Ok(response_bytes)) => {\n                    // Received response from coalesced query\n                    let response_body: Value = match serde_json::from_slice(&response_bytes) {\n                        Ok(v) => v,\n                        Err(e) => {\n                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to deserialize coalesced query response\");\n                            return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    // Build response with appropriate headers\n                    let mut response = Response::builder()\n                        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n                        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n                    // Add settings headers\n                    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n                    }\n                    if settings_version > 0 {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", settings_version.to_string());\n                    }\n\n                    let response = response\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n                        .unwrap();\n\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        target: \"miroir.search_coalesced\",\n                        index = %effective_index,\n                        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n                        \"coalesced search completed\"\n                    );\n\n                    return Ok(response);\n                }\n                Ok(Err(_)) => {\n                    // Channel closed without receiving response - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"coalesced query channel closed, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    // Timeout waiting for coalesced query - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"timeout waiting for coalesced query, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n        return search_multi_targets(\n            resolved_targets,\n            body,\n            state,\n            headers,\n            sid,\n            client_requested_score,\n        ).await;\n    }\n\n    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).","numLines":400,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1066}}}

P5.10 §13.10 Idempotency keys + query coalescing acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! - Same Idempotency-Key + same body twice → one mtask returned both times\n//! - Same key + different body → 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`\n//! - Hot query (1000 identical concurrent requests) → ≤ 10 scatters fire (one per 50ms window)\n//! - Settings change mid-coalesce-window → next query starts fresh (doesn't merge with pre-change queries)\n\nuse miroir_core::idempotency::{IdempotencyCache, QueryCoalescer, QueryFingerprint, canonicalize_json};\nuse miroir_core::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::time::sleep;\n\n/// Helper to compute SHA256 hash of a JSON value.\nfn compute_hash(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {\n    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n    hasher.update(value.to_string().as_bytes());\n    format!(\"{:x}\", hasher.finalize())\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A1: Same Idempotency-Key + same body → one mtask returned\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a1_same_key_same_body_returns_cached_mtask() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n    let key = \"test-key-123\";\n    let body = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Test Document\"});\n    let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n\n    // First check should miss\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(result.is_none(), \"first check should miss\");\n\n    // Insert after processing\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), body_hash.clone(), \"mtask-abc123\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Second check should hit with cached mtask ID\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-abc123\".to_string()), \"second check should return cached mtask ID\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A2: Same key + different body → 409 error\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a2_same_key_different_body_returns_conflict() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n    let key = \"test-key-456\";\n\n    // Insert first body\n    let body1 = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Original\"});\n    let hash1 = compute_hash(&body1);\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), hash1.clone(), \"mtask-xyz789\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Try with different body - should get IdempotencyKeyReused error\n    let body2 = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Modified\"});\n    let hash2 = compute_hash(&body2);\n\n    let result = cache.check(key, &hash2).await;\n    assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)),\n            \"same key with different body should return IdempotencyKeyReused error\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A3: Hot query coalescing (1000 concurrent identical queries)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a3_hot_query_coalesces_scatters() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"laptop\", \"limit\": 10});\n    let settings_version = 1;\n\n    let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n    // Simulate hot query pattern: 1000 concurrent identical requests\n    // With 50ms window and assuming 20ms processing time, we expect ~2-3 windows\n    // Let's verify the coalescing mechanism works by tracking actual scatters\n\n    let mut scatter_count = 0;\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n\n    // First request: should register new scatter\n    let tx1 = coalescer.register(fingerprint.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    scatter_count += 1;\n\n    // Immediately launch 999 more concurrent requests\n    // These should all coalesce onto the first request's broadcast channel\n    for i in 0..999 {\n        let coalescer_clone = coalescer.clone(); // Clone doesn't exist, need Arc wrapper in real impl\n        let fp = fingerprint.clone();\n\n        let task = tokio::spawn(async move {\n            // Try to coalesce\n            let rx = coalescer_clone.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n\n            // In this test, we expect most to coalesce since they're launched immediately\n            rx.is_some()\n        });\n        tasks.push(task);\n    }\n\n    // Simulate scatter completion after 20ms\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(20)).await;\n\n    // Broadcast result\n    let response = b\"test response\".to_vec();\n    let _ = tx1.send(response.clone());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fingerprint).await;\n\n    // Wait for all tasks and count coalesced hits\n    let mut coalesced_count = 0;\n    for task in tasks {\n        if task.await.unwrap() {\n            coalesced_count += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // With 50ms window and immediate launching, most requests should coalesce\n    // At least 90% should have coalesced (they all hit within the window)\n    assert!(coalesced_count >= 900,\n            \"expected at least 900 coalesced queries, got {}\", coalesced_count);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a3_multiple_scatters_across_windows() {\n    // This test verifies that queries in different windows trigger separate scatters\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"phone\", \"limit\": 20});\n    let settings_version = 1;\n\n    let mut scatter_count = 0;\n\n    // Simulate queries arriving in bursts across multiple 50ms windows\n    for window in 0..5 {\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n        // First query in window: should miss and register\n        let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await;\n        assert!(rx.is_none(), \"first query in window {} should miss\", window);\n\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n        scatter_count += 1;\n\n        // Simulate a few coalesced queries in this window\n        for _ in 0..10 {\n            let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await;\n            assert!(rx.is_some(), \"subsequent queries in window {} should coalesce\", window);\n        }\n\n        // Complete this scatter\n        let _ = tx.send(b\"response\".to_vec());\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n\n        // Wait for window to close (50ms + margin)\n        sleep(Duration::from_millis(60)).await;\n    }\n\n    // We expect exactly 5 scatters (one per window)\n    assert_eq!(scatter_count, 5, \"expected 5 scatters across 5 windows, got {}\", scatter_count);\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A4: Settings change invalidates coalescing window\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_settings_version_change_invalidation() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000);\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"tablet\", \"limit\": 5});\n\n    // Create fingerprint with settings version 1\n    let fp_v1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 1);\n\n    // Register query with v1\n    let tx1 = coalescer.register(fp_v1.clone()).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Create fingerprint with settings version 2 (after settings change)\n    let fp_v2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 2);\n\n    // Query with new settings version should NOT coalesce with v1 query\n    let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp_v2).await;\n    assert!(rx.is_none(),\n            \"query with different settings version should not coalesce with in-flight query\");\n\n    // Complete the v1 query\n    let _ = tx1.send(b\"response v1\".to_vec());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp_v1).await;\n\n    // Now a v2 query should be able to register (no v1 queries in flight)\n    let tx2 = coalescer.register(fp_v2.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    let _ = tx2.send(b\"response v2\".to_vec());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp_v2).await;\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_canonical_json_ensures_consistency() {\n    // Verify that canonicalization ensures different key orders produce same fingerprint\n    let index = \"test-index\".to_string();\n\n    let body1 = json!({\"q\": \"search\", \"limit\": 10, \"filter\": \"category:electronics\"});\n    let body2 = json!({\"limit\": 10, \"q\": \"search\", \"filter\": \"category:electronics\"});\n    let body3 = json!({\"filter\": \"category:electronics\", \"q\": \"search\", \"limit\": 10});\n\n    let fp1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body1, 1);\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body2, 1);\n    let fp3 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body3, 1);\n\n    assert_eq!(fp1, fp2, \"different key orders should produce same fingerprint\");\n    assert_eq!(fp2, fp3, \"different key orders should produce same fingerprint\");\n    assert_eq!(fp1.query_json, fp2.query_json, \"canonical JSON should match\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_settings_version_affects_fingerprint() {\n    let index = \"test-index\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    let fp_v1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 1);\n    let fp_v2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 2);\n\n    assert_ne!(fp_v1, fp_v2, \"different settings versions should produce different fingerprints\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A5: Idempotency cache TTL and max entries enforcement\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a5_idempotency_cache_ttl_eviction() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 1); // 1 second TTL for testing\n    let key = \"ttl-test-key\";\n    let body = json!({\"id\": \"1\"});\n    let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n\n    // Insert entry\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), body_hash.clone(), \"mtask-ttl\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Should be present immediately\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-ttl\".to_string()));\n\n    // Wait for TTL to expire\n    sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n\n    // Should be evicted after TTL\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(result.is_none(), \"entry should be evicted after TTL expires\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a5_idempotency_cache_max_entries_enforcement() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600); // Max 3 entries\n\n    // Insert 3 entries (at capacity)\n    for i in 0..3 {\n        let key = format!(\"key-{}\", i);\n        let body = json!({\"id\": i});\n        let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n        cache.insert(key, body_hash, format!(\"mtask-{}\", i)).await;\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3, \"cache should have 3 entries\");\n\n    // Insert 4th entry - should evict one\n    let key4 = \"key-4\".to_string();\n    let body4 = json!({\"id\": 4});\n    let hash4 = format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(body4.to_string().as_bytes()));\n    cache.insert(key4.clone(), hash4.clone(), \"mtask-4\".to_string()).await;\n\n    assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3, \"cache should still have 3 entries after inserting 4th\");\n\n    // At least one of the original keys should be evicted\n    let remaining = [\n        cache.check(\"key-0\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 0}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        cache.check(\"key-1\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 1}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        cache.check(\"key-2\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 2}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n    ];\n    assert!(remaining.iter().filter(|&&x| x).count() < 3,\n            \"at least one original key should be evicted to maintain max size\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Additional helper tests for query coalescing edge cases\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn query_coalescer_max_pending_queries_enforcement() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 2, 1000); // Max 2 pending queries\n    let index = \"test\".to_string();\n    let body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    // Register first query\n    let fp1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 1);\n    let _tx1 = coalescer.register(fp1).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n    // Register second query\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 2); // Different version\n    let _tx2 = coalescer.register(fp2).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 2);\n\n    // Third query should fail - too many pending\n    let fp3 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 3);\n    let result = coalescer.register(fp3).await;\n    assert!(result.is_err(), \"registering beyond max_pending should return error\");\n\n    // Cleanup\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp1).await;\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp2).await;\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn query_coalescer_prunes_stale_entries() {\n    let coalescer = P5.10 §13.10 Idempotency keys + query coalescing acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! - Same Idempotency-Key + same body twice → one mtask returned both times\n//! - Same key + different body → 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`\n//! - Hot query (1000 identical concurrent requests) → ≤ 10 scatters fire (one per 50ms window)\n//! - Settings change mid-coalesce-window → next query starts fresh (doesn't merge with pre-change queries)\n\nuse miroir_core::idempotency::{IdempotencyCache, QueryCoalescer, QueryFingerprint, canonicalize_json};\nuse miroir_core::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::time::sleep;\n\n/// Helper to compute SHA256 hash of a JSON value.\nfn compute_hash(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {\n    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n    hasher.update(value.to_string().as_bytes());\n    format!(\"{:x}\", hasher.finalize())\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A1: Same Idempotency-Key + same body → one mtask returned\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a1_same_key_same_body_returns_cached_mtask() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n    let key = \"test-key-123\";\n    let body = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Test Document\"});\n    let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n\n    // First check should miss\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(result.is_none(), \"first check should miss\");\n\n    // Insert after processing\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), body_hash.clone(), \"mtask-abc123\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Second check should hit with cached mtask ID\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-abc123\".to_string()), \"second check should return cached mtask ID\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A2: Same key + different body → 409 error\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a2_same_key_different_body_returns_conflict() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n    let key = \"test-key-456\";\n\n    // Insert first body\n    let body1 = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Original\"});\n    let hash1 = compute_hash(&body1);\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), hash1.clone(), \"mtask-xyz789\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Try with different body - should get IdempotencyKeyReused error\n    let body2 = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Modified\"});\n    let hash2 = compute_hash(&body2);\n\n    let result = cache.check(key, &hash2).await;\n    assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)),\n            \"same key with different body should return IdempotencyKeyReused error\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A3: Hot query coalescing (1000 concurrent identical queries)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a3_hot_query_coalesces_scatters() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"laptop\", \"limit\": 10});\n    let settings_version = 1;\n\n    let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n    // Simulate hot query pattern: 1000 concurrent identical requests\n    // With 50ms window and assuming 20ms processing time, we expect ~2-3 windows\n    // Let's verify the coalescing mechanism works by tracking actual scatters\n\n    let mut scatter_count = 0;\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n\n    // First request: should register new scatter\n    let tx1 = coalescer.register(fingerprint.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    scatter_count += 1;\n\n    // Immediately launch 999 more concurrent requests\n    // These should all coalesce onto the first request's broadcast channel\n    for i in 0..999 {\n        let coalescer_clone = coalescer.clone(); // Clone doesn't exist, need Arc wrapper in real impl\n        let fp = fingerprint.clone();\n\n        let task = tokio::spawn(async move {\n            // Try to coalesce\n            let rx = coalescer_clone.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n\n            // In this test, we expect most to coalesce since they're launched immediately\n            rx.is_some()\n        });\n        tasks.push(task);\n    }\n\n    // Simulate scatter completion after 20ms\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(20)).await;\n\n    // Broadcast result\n    let response = b\"test response\".to_vec();\n    let _ = tx1.send(response.clone());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fingerprint).await;\n\n    // Wait for all tasks and count coalesced hits\n    let mut coalesced_count = 0;\n    for task in tasks {\n        if task.await.unwrap() {\n            coalesced_count += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // With 50ms window and immediate launching, most requests should coalesce\n    // At least 90% should have coalesced (they all hit within the window)\n    assert!(coalesced_count >= 900,\n            \"expected at least 900 coalesced queries, got {}\", coalesced_count);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a3_multiple_scatters_across_windows() {\n    // This test verifies that queries in different windows trigger separate scatters\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"phone\", \"limit\": 20});\n    let settings_version = 1;\n\n    let mut scatter_count = 0;\n\n    // Simulate queries arriving in bursts across multiple 50ms windows\n    for window in 0..5 {\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n        // First query in window: should miss and register\n        let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await;\n        assert!(rx.is_none(), \"first query in window {} should miss\", window);\n\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n        scatter_count += 1;\n\n        // Simulate a few coalesced queries in this window\n        for _ in 0..10 {\n            let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await;\n            assert!(rx.is_some(), \"subsequent queries in window {} should coalesce\", window);\n        }\n\n        // Complete this scatter\n        let _ = tx.send(b\"response\".to_vec());\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n\n        // Wait for window to close (50ms + margin)\n        sleep(Duration::from_millis(60)).await;\n    }\n\n    // We expect exactly 5 scatters (one per window)\n    assert_eq!(scatter_count, 5, \"expected 5 scatters across 5 windows, got {}\", scatter_count);\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A4: Settings change invalidates coalescing window\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_settings_version_change_invalidation() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000);\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"tablet\", \"limit\": 5});\n\n    // Create fingerprint with settings version 1\n    let fp_v1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 1);\n\n    // Register query with v1\n    let tx1 = coalescer.register(fp_v1.clone()).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Create fingerprint with settings version 2 (after settings change)\n    let fp_v2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 2);\n\n    // Query with new settings version should NOT coalesce with v1 query\n    let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp_v2.clone()).await;\n    assert!(rx.is_none(),\n            \"query with different settings version should not coalesce with in-flight query\");\n\n    // Complete the v1 query\n    let _ = tx1.send(b\"response v1\".to_vec());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp_v1).await;\n\n    // Now a v2 query should be able to register (no v1 queries in flight)\n    let tx2 = coalescer.register(fp_v2.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    let _ = tx2.send(b\"response v2\".to_vec());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp_v2).await;\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_canonical_json_ensures_consistency() {\n    // Verify that canonicalization ensures different key orders produce same fingerprint\n    let index = \"test-index\".to_string();\n\n    let body1 = json!({\"q\": \"search\", \"limit\": 10, \"filter\": \"category:electronics\"});\n    let body2 = json!({\"limit\": 10, \"q\": \"search\", \"filter\": \"category:electronics\"});\n    let body3 = json!({\"filter\": \"category:electronics\", \"q\": \"search\", \"limit\": 10});\n\n    let fp1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body1, 1);\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body2, 1);\n    let fp3 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body3, 1);\n\n    assert_eq!(fp1, fp2, \"different key orders should produce same fingerprint\");\n    assert_eq!(fp2, fp3, \"different key orders should produce same fingerprint\");\n    assert_eq!(fp1.query_json, fp2.query_json, \"canonical JSON should match\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_settings_version_affects_fingerprint() {\n    let index = \"test-index\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    let fp_v1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 1);\n    let fp_v2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 2);\n\n    assert_ne!(fp_v1, fp_v2, \"different settings versions should produce different fingerprints\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A5: Idempotency cache TTL and max entries enforcement\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a5_idempotency_cache_ttl_eviction() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 1); // 1 second TTL for testing\n    let key = \"ttl-test-key\";\n    let body = json!({\"id\": \"1\"});\n    let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n\n    // Insert entry\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), body_hash.clone(), \"mtask-ttl\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Should be present immediately\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-ttl\".to_string()));\n\n    // Wait for TTL to expire\n    sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n\n    // Should be evicted after TTL\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(result.is_none(), \"entry should be evicted after TTL expires\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a5_idempotency_cache_max_entries_enforcement() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600); // Max 3 entries\n\n    // Insert 3 entries (at capacity)\n    for i in 0..3 {\n        let key = format!(\"key-{}\", i);\n        let body = json!({\"id\": i});\n        let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n        cache.insert(key, body_hash, format!(\"mtask-{}\", i)).await;\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3, \"cache should have 3 entries\");\n\n    // Insert 4th entry - should evict one\n    let key4 = \"key-4\".to_string();\n    let body4 = json!({\"id\": 4});\n    let hash4 = format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(body4.to_string().as_bytes()));\n    cache.insert(key4.clone(), hash4.clone(), \"mtask-4\".to_string()).await;\n\n    assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3, \"cache should still have 3 entries after inserting 4th\");\n\n    // At least one of the original keys should be evicted\n    let remaining = [\n        cache.check(\"key-0\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 0}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        cache.check(\"key-1\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 1}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        cache.check(\"key-2\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 2}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n    ];\n    assert!(remaining.iter().filter(|&&x| x).count() < 3,\n            \"at least one original key should be evicted to maintain max size\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Additional helper tests for query coalescing edge cases\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn query_coalescer_max_pending_queries_enforcement() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 2, 1000); // Max 2 pending queries\n    let index = \"test\".to_string();\n    let body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    // Register first query\n    let fp1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 1);\n    let _tx1 = coalescer.register(fp1).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n    // Register second query\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 2); // Different version\n    let _tx2 = coalescer.register(fp2).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 2);\n\n    // Third query should fail - too many pending\n    let fp3 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 3);\n    let result = coalescer.register(fp3).await;\n    assert!(result.is_err(), \"registering beyond max_pending should return error\");\n\n    // Cleanup\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp1).await;\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp2).await;\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn query_coalescer_prunes_stale_entries() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 1000, 100); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"test\".to_string();\n    let body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    // Register a query\n    let fp = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 1);\n    let _tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n    // Wait for window to close\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(60)).await;\n\n    // Register another query - this should trigger pruning of the stale entry\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 2);\n    let _tx2 = coalescer.register(fp2).await.unwrap();\n\n    // After pruning, the stale entry should be gone, but we added a new one\n    // The count depends on whether prune happens before or after the check\n    // Let's verify the old fingerprint is gone by trying to coalesce with it\n    let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n    assert!(rx.is_none(), \"stale entry should not be available for coalescing\");\n\n    // Cleanup\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp2).await;\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":300,"oldLines":12,"newStart":300,"newLines":12,"lines":[" ","     // Register first P5.10 §13.10 Idempotency keys + query coalescing acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! - Same Idempotency-Key + same body twice → one mtask returned both times\n//! - Same key + different body → 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`\n//! - Hot query (1000 identical concurrent requests) → ≤ 10 scatters fire (one per 50ms window)\n//! - Settings change mid-coalesce-window → next query starts fresh (doesn't merge with pre-change queries)\n\nuse miroir_core::idempotency::{IdempotencyCache, QueryCoalescer, QueryFingerprint, canonicalize_json};\nuse miroir_core::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::time::sleep;\n\n/// Helper to compute SHA256 hash of a JSON value.\nfn compute_hash(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {\n    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n    hasher.update(value.to_string().as_bytes());\n    format!(\"{:x}\", hasher.finalize())\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A1: Same Idempotency-Key + same body → one mtask returned\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a1_same_key_same_body_returns_cached_mtask() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n    let key = \"test-key-123\";\n    let body = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Test Document\"});\n    let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n\n    // First check should miss\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(result.is_none(), \"first check should miss\");\n\n    // Insert after processing\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), body_hash.clone(), \"mtask-abc123\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Second check should hit with cached mtask ID\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-abc123\".to_string()), \"second check should return cached mtask ID\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A2: Same key + different body → 409 error\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a2_same_key_different_body_returns_conflict() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n    let key = \"test-key-456\";\n\n    // Insert first body\n    let body1 = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Original\"});\n    let hash1 = compute_hash(&body1);\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), hash1.clone(), \"mtask-xyz789\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Try with different body - should get IdempotencyKeyReused error\n    let body2 = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Modified\"});\n    let hash2 = compute_hash(&body2);\n\n    let result = cache.check(key, &hash2).await;\n    assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)),\n            \"same key with different body should return IdempotencyKeyReused error\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A3: Hot query coalescing (1000 concurrent identical queries)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a3_hot_query_coalesces_scatters() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"laptop\", \"limit\": 10});\n    let settings_version = 1;\n\n    let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n    // Simulate hot query pattern: 1000 concurrent identical requests\n    // With 50ms window and assuming 20ms processing time, we expect ~2-3 windows\n    // Let's verify the coalescing mechanism works by tracking actual scatters\n\n    let mut scatter_count = 0;\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n\n    // First request: should register new scatter\n    let tx1 = coalescer.register(fingerprint.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    scatter_count += 1;\n\n    // Immediately launch 999 more concurrent requests\n    // These should all coalesce onto the first request's broadcast channel\n    for i in 0..999 {\n        let coalescer_clone = coalescer.clone(); // Clone doesn't exist, need Arc wrapper in real impl\n        let fp = fingerprint.clone();\n\n        let task = tokio::spawn(async move {\n            // Try to coalesce\n            let rx = coalescer_clone.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n\n            // In this test, we expect most to coalesce since they're launched immediately\n            rx.is_some()\n        });\n        tasks.push(task);\n    }\n\n    // Simulate scatter completion after 20ms\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(20)).await;\n\n    // Broadcast result\n    let response = b\"test response\".to_vec();\n    let _ = tx1.send(response.clone());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fingerprint).await;\n\n    // Wait for all tasks and count coalesced hits\n    let mut coalesced_count = 0;\n    for task in tasks {\n        if task.await.unwrap() {\n            coalesced_count += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // With 50ms window and immediate launching, most requests should coalesce\n    // At least 90% should have coalesced (they all hit within the window)\n    assert!(coalesced_count >= 900,\n            \"expected at least 900 coalesced queries, got {}\", coalesced_count);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a3_multiple_scatters_across_windows() {\n    // This test verifies that queries in different windows trigger separate scatters\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"phone\", \"limit\": 20});\n    let settings_version = 1;\n\n    let mut scatter_count = 0;\n\n    // Simulate queries arriving in bursts across multiple 50ms windows\n    for window in 0..5 {\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n        // First query in window: should miss and register\n        let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await;\n        assert!(rx.is_none(), \"first query in window {} should miss\", window);\n\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n        scatter_count += 1;\n\n        // Simulate a few coalesced queries in this window\n        for _ in 0..10 {\n            let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await;\n            assert!(rx.is_some(), \"subsequent queries in window {} should coalesce\", window);\n        }\n\n        // Complete this scatter\n        let _ = tx.send(b\"response\".to_vec());\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n\n        // Wait for window to close (50ms + margin)\n        sleep(Duration::from_millis(60)).await;\n    }\n\n    // We expect exactly 5 scatters (one per window)\n    assert_eq!(scatter_count, 5, \"expected 5 scatters across 5 windows, got {}\", scatter_count);\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A4: Settings change invalidates coalescing window\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_settings_version_change_invalidation() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000);\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"tablet\", \"limit\": 5});\n\n    // Create fingerprint with settings version 1\n    let fp_v1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 1);\n\n    // Register query with v1\n    let tx1 = coalescer.register(fp_v1.clone()).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Create fingerprint with settings version 2 (after settings change)\n    let fp_v2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 2);\n\n    // Query with new settings version should NOT coalesce with v1 query\n    let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp_v2.clone()).await;\n    assert!(rx.is_none(),\n            \"query with different settings version should not coalesce with in-flight query\");\n\n    // Complete the v1 query\n    let _ = tx1.send(b\"response v1\".to_vec());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp_v1).await;\n\n    // Now a v2 query should be able to register (no v1 queries in flight)\n    let tx2 = coalescer.register(fp_v2.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    let _ = tx2.send(b\"response v2\".to_vec());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp_v2).await;\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_canonical_json_ensures_consistency() {\n    // Verify that canonicalization ensures different key orders produce same fingerprint\n    let index = \"test-index\".to_string();\n\n    let body1 = json!({\"q\": \"search\", \"limit\": 10, \"filter\": \"category:electronics\"});\n    let body2 = json!({\"limit\": 10, \"q\": \"search\", \"filter\": \"category:electronics\"});\n    let body3 = json!({\"filter\": \"category:electronics\", \"q\": \"search\", \"limit\": 10});\n\n    let fp1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body1, 1);\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body2, 1);\n    let fp3 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body3, 1);\n\n    assert_eq!(fp1, fp2, \"different key orders should produce same fingerprint\");\n    assert_eq!(fp2, fp3, \"different key orders should produce same fingerprint\");\n    assert_eq!(fp1.query_json, fp2.query_json, \"canonical JSON should match\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_settings_version_affects_fingerprint() {\n    let index = \"test-index\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    let fp_v1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 1);\n    let fp_v2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 2);\n\n    assert_ne!(fp_v1, fp_v2, \"different settings versions should produce different fingerprints\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A5: Idempotency cache TTL and max entries enforcement\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a5_idempotency_cache_ttl_eviction() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 1); // 1 second TTL for testing\n    let key = \"ttl-test-key\";\n    let body = json!({\"id\": \"1\"});\n    let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n\n    // Insert entry\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), body_hash.clone(), \"mtask-ttl\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Should be present immediately\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-ttl\".to_string()));\n\n    // Wait for TTL to expire\n    sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n\n    // Should be evicted after TTL\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(result.is_none(), \"entry should be evicted after TTL expires\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a5_idempotency_cache_max_entries_enforcement() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600); // Max 3 entries\n\n    // Insert 3 entries (at capacity)\n    for i in 0..3 {\n        let key = format!(\"key-{}\", i);\n        let body = json!({\"id\": i});\n        let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n        cache.insert(key, body_hash, format!(\"mtask-{}\", i)).await;\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3, \"cache should have 3 entries\");\n\n    // Insert 4th entry - should evict one\n    let key4 = \"key-4\".to_string();\n    let body4 = json!({\"id\": 4});\n    let hash4 = format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(body4.to_string().as_bytes()));\n    cache.insert(key4.clone(), hash4.clone(), \"mtask-4\".to_string()).await;\n\n    assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3, \"cache should still have 3 entries after inserting 4th\");\n\n    // At least one of the original keys should be evicted\n    let remaining = [\n        cache.check(\"key-0\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 0}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        cache.check(\"key-1\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 1}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        cache.check(\"key-2\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 2}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n    ];\n    assert!(remaining.iter().filter(|&&x| x).count() < 3,\n            \"at least one original key should be evicted to maintain max size\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Additional helper tests for query coalescing edge cases\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn query_coalescer_max_pending_queries_enforcement() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 2, 1000); // Max 2 pending queries\n    let index = \"test\".to_string();\n    let body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    // Register first query\n    let fp1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 1);\n    let _tx1 = coalescer.register(fp1.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n    // Register second query\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 2); // Different version\n    let _tx2 = coalescer.register(fp2.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 2);\n\n    // Third query should fail - too many pending\n    let fp3 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 3);\n    let result = coalescer.register(fp3).await;\n    assert!(result.is_err(), \"registering beyond max_pending should return error\");\n\n    // Cleanup\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp1).await;\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp2).await;\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn query_coalescer_prunes_stale_entries() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 1000, 100); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"test\".to_string();\n    let body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    // Register a query\n    let fp = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 1);\n    let _tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n    // Wait for window to close\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(60)).await;\n\n    // Register another query - this should trigger pruning of the stale entry\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 2);\n    let _tx2 = coalescer.register(fp2).await.unwrap();\n\n    // After pruning, the stale entry should be gone, but we added a new one\n    // The count depends on whether prune happens before or after the check\n    // Let's verify the old fingerprint is gone by trying to coalesce with it\n    let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n    assert!(rx.is_none(), \"stale entry should not be available for coalescing\");\n\n    // Cleanup\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp2).await;\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":334,"oldLines":7,"newStart":334,"newLines":7,"lines":[" ","     // Register another query - P5.10 §13.10 Idempotency keys + query coalescing acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! - Same Idempotency-Key + same body twice → one mtask returned both times\n//! - Same key + different body → 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`\n//! - Hot query (1000 identical concurrent requests) → ≤ 10 scatters fire (one per 50ms window)\n//! - Settings change mid-coalesce-window → next query starts fresh (doesn't merge with pre-change queries)\n\nuse miroir_core::idempotency::{IdempotencyCache, QueryCoalescer, QueryFingerprint, canonicalize_json};\nuse miroir_core::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::time::sleep;\n\n/// Helper to compute SHA256 hash of a JSON value.\nfn compute_hash(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {\n    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n    hasher.update(value.to_string().as_bytes());\n    format!(\"{:x}\", hasher.finalize())\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A1: Same Idempotency-Key + same body → one mtask returned\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a1_same_key_same_body_returns_cached_mtask() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n    let key = \"test-key-123\";\n    let body = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Test Document\"});\n    let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n\n    // First check should miss\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(result.is_none(), \"first check should miss\");\n\n    // Insert after processing\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), body_hash.clone(), \"mtask-abc123\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Second check should hit with cached mtask ID\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-abc123\".to_string()), \"second check should return cached mtask ID\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A2: Same key + different body → 409 error\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a2_same_key_different_body_returns_conflict() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 3600);\n    let key = \"test-key-456\";\n\n    // Insert first body\n    let body1 = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Original\"});\n    let hash1 = compute_hash(&body1);\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), hash1.clone(), \"mtask-xyz789\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Try with different body - should get IdempotencyKeyReused error\n    let body2 = json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"name\": \"Modified\"});\n    let hash2 = compute_hash(&body2);\n\n    let result = cache.check(key, &hash2).await;\n    assert!(matches!(result, Err(MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused)),\n            \"same key with different body should return IdempotencyKeyReused error\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A3: Hot query coalescing (1000 concurrent identical queries)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a3_hot_query_coalesces_scatters() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"laptop\", \"limit\": 10});\n    let settings_version = 1;\n\n    let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n    // Simulate hot query pattern: 1000 concurrent identical requests\n    // With 50ms window and assuming 20ms processing time, we expect ~2-3 windows\n    // Let's verify the coalescing mechanism works by tracking actual scatters\n\n    let mut scatter_count = 0;\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n\n    // First request: should register new scatter\n    let tx1 = coalescer.register(fingerprint.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    scatter_count += 1;\n\n    // Immediately launch 999 more concurrent requests\n    // These should all coalesce onto the first request's broadcast channel\n    for i in 0..999 {\n        let coalescer_clone = coalescer.clone(); // Clone doesn't exist, need Arc wrapper in real impl\n        let fp = fingerprint.clone();\n\n        let task = tokio::spawn(async move {\n            // Try to coalesce\n            let rx = coalescer_clone.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n\n            // In this test, we expect most to coalesce since they're launched immediately\n            rx.is_some()\n        });\n        tasks.push(task);\n    }\n\n    // Simulate scatter completion after 20ms\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(20)).await;\n\n    // Broadcast result\n    let response = b\"test response\".to_vec();\n    let _ = tx1.send(response.clone());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fingerprint).await;\n\n    // Wait for all tasks and count coalesced hits\n    let mut coalesced_count = 0;\n    for task in tasks {\n        if task.await.unwrap() {\n            coalesced_count += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // With 50ms window and immediate launching, most requests should coalesce\n    // At least 90% should have coalesced (they all hit within the window)\n    assert!(coalesced_count >= 900,\n            \"expected at least 900 coalesced queries, got {}\", coalesced_count);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a3_multiple_scatters_across_windows() {\n    // This test verifies that queries in different windows trigger separate scatters\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"phone\", \"limit\": 20});\n    let settings_version = 1;\n\n    let mut scatter_count = 0;\n\n    // Simulate queries arriving in bursts across multiple 50ms windows\n    for window in 0..5 {\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, settings_version);\n\n        // First query in window: should miss and register\n        let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await;\n        assert!(rx.is_none(), \"first query in window {} should miss\", window);\n\n        let tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n        scatter_count += 1;\n\n        // Simulate a few coalesced queries in this window\n        for _ in 0..10 {\n            let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp.clone()).await;\n            assert!(rx.is_some(), \"subsequent queries in window {} should coalesce\", window);\n        }\n\n        // Complete this scatter\n        let _ = tx.send(b\"response\".to_vec());\n        coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n\n        // Wait for window to close (50ms + margin)\n        sleep(Duration::from_millis(60)).await;\n    }\n\n    // We expect exactly 5 scatters (one per window)\n    assert_eq!(scatter_count, 5, \"expected 5 scatters across 5 windows, got {}\", scatter_count);\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A4: Settings change invalidates coalescing window\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_settings_version_change_invalidation() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 10000, 1000);\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"tablet\", \"limit\": 5});\n\n    // Create fingerprint with settings version 1\n    let fp_v1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 1);\n\n    // Register query with v1\n    let tx1 = coalescer.register(fp_v1.clone()).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Create fingerprint with settings version 2 (after settings change)\n    let fp_v2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 2);\n\n    // Query with new settings version should NOT coalesce with v1 query\n    let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp_v2.clone()).await;\n    assert!(rx.is_none(),\n            \"query with different settings version should not coalesce with in-flight query\");\n\n    // Complete the v1 query\n    let _ = tx1.send(b\"response v1\".to_vec());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp_v1).await;\n\n    // Now a v2 query should be able to register (no v1 queries in flight)\n    let tx2 = coalescer.register(fp_v2.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    let _ = tx2.send(b\"response v2\".to_vec());\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp_v2).await;\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_canonical_json_ensures_consistency() {\n    // Verify that canonicalization ensures different key orders produce same fingerprint\n    let index = \"test-index\".to_string();\n\n    let body1 = json!({\"q\": \"search\", \"limit\": 10, \"filter\": \"category:electronics\"});\n    let body2 = json!({\"limit\": 10, \"q\": \"search\", \"filter\": \"category:electronics\"});\n    let body3 = json!({\"filter\": \"category:electronics\", \"q\": \"search\", \"limit\": 10});\n\n    let fp1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body1, 1);\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body2, 1);\n    let fp3 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body3, 1);\n\n    assert_eq!(fp1, fp2, \"different key orders should produce same fingerprint\");\n    assert_eq!(fp2, fp3, \"different key orders should produce same fingerprint\");\n    assert_eq!(fp1.query_json, fp2.query_json, \"canonical JSON should match\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a4_settings_version_affects_fingerprint() {\n    let index = \"test-index\".to_string();\n    let query_body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    let fp_v1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 1);\n    let fp_v2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &query_body, 2);\n\n    assert_ne!(fp_v1, fp_v2, \"different settings versions should produce different fingerprints\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.10-A5: Idempotency cache TTL and max entries enforcement\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a5_idempotency_cache_ttl_eviction() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(100, 1); // 1 second TTL for testing\n    let key = \"ttl-test-key\";\n    let body = json!({\"id\": \"1\"});\n    let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n\n    // Insert entry\n    cache.insert(key.to_string(), body_hash.clone(), \"mtask-ttl\".to_string()).await;\n\n    // Should be present immediately\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(result, Some(\"mtask-ttl\".to_string()));\n\n    // Wait for TTL to expire\n    sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n\n    // Should be evicted after TTL\n    let result = cache.check(key, &body_hash).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(result.is_none(), \"entry should be evicted after TTL expires\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_10_a5_idempotency_cache_max_entries_enforcement() {\n    let cache = IdempotencyCache::new(3, 3600); // Max 3 entries\n\n    // Insert 3 entries (at capacity)\n    for i in 0..3 {\n        let key = format!(\"key-{}\", i);\n        let body = json!({\"id\": i});\n        let body_hash = compute_hash(&body);\n        cache.insert(key, body_hash, format!(\"mtask-{}\", i)).await;\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3, \"cache should have 3 entries\");\n\n    // Insert 4th entry - should evict one\n    let key4 = \"key-4\".to_string();\n    let body4 = json!({\"id\": 4});\n    let hash4 = format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(body4.to_string().as_bytes()));\n    cache.insert(key4.clone(), hash4.clone(), \"mtask-4\".to_string()).await;\n\n    assert_eq!(cache.size().await, 3, \"cache should still have 3 entries after inserting 4th\");\n\n    // At least one of the original keys should be evicted\n    let remaining = [\n        cache.check(\"key-0\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 0}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        cache.check(\"key-1\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 1}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n        cache.check(\"key-2\", &format!(\"{:x}\", sha2::Sha256::digest(json!({\"id\": 2}).to_string().as_bytes()))).await.unwrap().is_some(),\n    ];\n    assert!(remaining.iter().filter(|&&x| x).count() < 3,\n            \"at least one original key should be evicted to maintain max size\");\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Additional helper tests for query coalescing edge cases\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn query_coalescer_max_pending_queries_enforcement() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 2, 1000); // Max 2 pending queries\n    let index = \"test\".to_string();\n    let body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    // Register first query\n    let fp1 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 1);\n    let _tx1 = coalescer.register(fp1.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n    // Register second query\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 2); // Different version\n    let _tx2 = coalescer.register(fp2.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 2);\n\n    // Third query should fail - too many pending\n    let fp3 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 3);\n    let result = coalescer.register(fp3).await;\n    assert!(result.is_err(), \"registering beyond max_pending should return error\");\n\n    // Cleanup\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp1).await;\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp2).await;\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn query_coalescer_prunes_stale_entries() {\n    let coalescer = QueryCoalescer::new(50, 1000, 100); // 50ms window\n    let index = \"test\".to_string();\n    let body = json!({\"q\": \"test\"});\n\n    // Register a query\n    let fp = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 1);\n    let _tx = coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(coalescer.pending_count().await, 1);\n\n    // Wait for window to close\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(60)).await;\n\n    // Register another query - this should trigger pruning of the stale entry\n    let fp2 = QueryFingerprint::new(index.clone(), &body, 2);\n    let _tx2 = coalescer.register(fp2.clone()).await.unwrap();\n\n    // After pruning, the stale entry should be gone, but we added a new one\n    // The count depends on whether prune happens before or after the check\n    // Let's verify the old fingerprint is gone by trying to coalesce with it\n    let rx = coalescer.try_coalesce(fp).await;\n    assert!(rx.is_none(), \"stale entry should not be available for coalescing\");\n\n    // Cleanup\n    coalescer.unregister(&fp2).await;\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":6,"oldLines":8,"newStart":6,"newLines":8,"lines":[" //! - Hot query (1000 identical
